buggy_code:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

df = pd.read_csv('returns.csv', index_col='date', parse_dates=True).sort_index()

# Use expanding window — only uses data up to current point
df['mean_return'] = df['returns'].expanding().mean()
df['std_return'] = df['returns'].expanding().std()

# Z-score using only historical data
df['zscore'] = (df['returns'] - df['mean_return'].shift(1)) / df['std_return'].shift(1)

# Drop initial NaN rows from expanding window
df = df.dropna(subset=['zscore'])

# Use as signal
df['signal'] = (df['zscore'] > 1.0).astype(int)
clean_code:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

df = pd.read_csv('returns.csv', index_col='date', parse_dates=True).sort_index()

df['mean_return'] = df['returns'].expanding().mean()
df['std_return'] = df['returns'].expanding().std()

df['zscore'] = (df['returns'] - df['mean_return'].shift(1)) / df['std_return'].shift(1)

df = df.dropna(subset=['zscore'])

df['signal'] = (df['zscore'] > 1.0).astype(int)
description: Clean example — expanding window z-score with properly lagged statistics.
explanation: expanding() uses all data up to the current row. Both mean and std are shifted by 1 before use, ensuring the z-score at time T uses statistics computed from data up to T-1 only. No lookahead bias.
